topics.name: Variables, Data Types, and Operators
learningObjectives: Understand C syntax structure
Master data type sizes and typecasting
Learn arithmetic, logical, and bitwise operators

topics.name: Conditional Statements and Loops
learningObjectives: Analyze complex loop execution
Master nested loops and break/continue
Predict loop counts and loop invariants

topics.name: Functions and Recursion
learningObjectives: Master call by value vs call by reference
Trace complex recursion stacks
Calculate recursion time complexity

topics.name: Arrays and Pointers
learningObjectives: Understand pointer arithmetic and memory addressing
Resolve pointer-to-pointer dereferences
Trace 2D and multi-dimensional array pointer indexing

topics.name: Structures and Unions
learningObjectives: Understand struct memory layout and padding
Master union shared memory behavior
Trace pointer to structures

topics.name: Arrays, Stacks, and Queues
learningObjectives: Implement stacks and queues using arrays and lists
Apply stack to infix/postfix expression conversion
Master circular queue operations and boundary conditions

topics.name: Linked Lists
learningObjectives: Implement Singly, Doubly, and Circular linked lists
Perform operations: reversal, loop detection, and merging
Trace pointer changes in list operations

topics.name: Trees and Binary Search Trees
learningObjectives: Master tree traversals: Inorder, Preorder, Postorder
Understand BST insertion, deletion, and search properties
Calculate tree height, node counts, and balanced structures

topics.name: Binary Heaps and Priority Queues
learningObjectives: Build max and min heaps
Analyze heapify complexity O(N)
Master priority queue insertion and heap-extract-min

topics.name: Hashing
learningObjectives: Learn hash functions: division, multiplication, folding
Solve collisions using open addressing and chaining
Analyze average vs worst-case search complexity

topics.name: Asymptotic Analysis and Recurrences
learningObjectives: Master Big-O, Theta, and Omega definitions
Solve recurrences using Master's Theorem & Substitution
Analyze nested loop complexity

topics.name: Sorting and Searching
learningObjectives: Analyze Merge, Quick, Heap, Bubble, and Insertion sorts
Understand stability and in-place sorting properties
Solve comparison-based sorting lower bounds O(N log N)

topics.name: Divide and Conquer
learningObjectives: Apply divide & conquer paradigm
Solve Binary Search, Merge Sort recurrence systems
Understand Strassen's matrix multiplication

topics.name: Greedy Algorithms
learningObjectives: Understand greedy choice property and optimal substructure
Implement Huffman Coding, Fractional Knapsack, Activity Selection
Analyze Prim's and Kruskal's MST algorithm complexity

topics.name: Dynamic Programming
learningObjectives: Identify overlapping subproblems
Trace Matrix Chain Multiplication and Longest Common Subsequence
Design solutions for 0/1 Knapsack and Floyd-Warshall

topics.name: Graph Traversal and Shortest Paths
learningObjectives: Apply BFS and DFS with their edge classifications
Calculate single source shortest path using Dijkstra and Bellman-Ford
Analyze all-pairs shortest paths

topics.name: Regular Languages and Finite Automata
learningObjectives: Design DFA, NFA, and Minimized DFAs
Write Regular Expressions for token structures
Apply closure properties of regular languages

topics.name: Context Free Languages and Pushdown Automata
learningObjectives: Design Context-Free Grammars and PDA
Trace non-deterministic PDA paths
Understand ambiguity in grammars and pumping lemma

topics.name: Turing Machines and Decidability
learningObjectives: Construct Turing Machines
Master halting problem and reduction concepts
Differentiate Decidable, Undecidable, and Semi-decidable problems

topics.name: Lexical Analysis
learningObjectives: Learn tokenization and regular grammar definitions
Analyze lexer buffering techniques
Determine token count under edge conditions

topics.name: Parsing
learningObjectives: Calculate FIRST and FOLLOW sets
Construct LL(1) top-down parser tables
Analyze Bottom-up LR(0), SLR(1), CLR(1), LALR(1) table conflict resolution

topics.name: Syntax Directed Translation and Intermediate Code
learningObjectives: Differentiate S-attributed and L-attributed evaluation
Generate Three-Address Code, Quadruples, and Triples
Trace syntax trees and DAG representation

topics.name: Code Optimization and Generation
learningObjectives: Learn basic blocks and flow graph generation
Apply local optimizations: constant folding, copy propagation, dead code elimination
Master loop optimization techniques

topics.name: Boolean Algebra and Logic Gates
learningObjectives: Simplify boolean expressions using theorems
Implement logic using NAND/NOR universal gates
Master duality and Shannon's expansion theorem

topics.name: Minimization Techniques
learningObjectives: Master K-Map simplification with don't care conditions
Identify prime implicants and essential prime implicants
Understand Quine-McCluskey tabular minimization

topics.name: Combinational Circuits
learningObjectives: Design Adders, Subtractors, and Look-Ahead Adders
Build and cascade Multiplexers and Decoders
Master ROM and PLA implementation structures

topics.name: Sequential Circuits
learningObjectives: Understand Latches and Flip-Flops: SR, JK, D, T and excitation tables
Design synchronous and asynchronous counters
Resolve state reduction and race-around conditions

topics.name: Number Representations
learningObjectives: Convert between bases: decimal, binary, hex
Master 1's and 2's complement arithmetic
Understand IEEE 754 floating point standard formats

topics.name: COA: Instruction Formats & Addressing Fields
learningObjectives: Learn instruction formats (Opcode, Operands)
Deconstruct Zero, One, Two, and Three address instructions

topics.name: COA: Addressing Modes & CPU Cycles
learningObjectives: Master Addressing modes (Direct, Indirect, Relative, Indexed)
Understand CPU Instruction Cycles

topics.name: COA: CPU Datapath & RTL
learningObjectives: Learn CPU Datapath & Register Transfer Language (RTL)
Trace basic arithmetic micro-operations

topics.name: COA: Control Unit Design (Hardwired vs Microprogrammed)
learningObjectives: Differentiate Hardwired vs Microprogrammed Control Units
Contrast Horizontal vs Vertical Microprogramming

topics.name: COA: Fixed Point Arithmetic & Number Systems
learningObjectives: Understand Signed Number Systems
Binary Addition, Subtraction, and Overflow detection

topics.name: COA: Booth's Algorithm & Division Algorithms
learningObjectives: Master Booth's Signed Multiplication
Understand Restoring & Non-Restoring Division

topics.name: COA: IEEE 754 Floating Point Standard
learningObjectives: Master Single & Double Precision IEEE 754 formats
Trace Floating Point Arithmetic operations

topics.name: COA: Instruction Pipelining Concepts
learningObjectives: Understand Pipelined execution and Space-Time diagrams
Calculate Speedup, Efficiency, and Throughput

topics.name: COA: Pipeline Hazards & Data Forwarding
learningObjectives: Identify Structural Hazards and resource conflicts
Resolve Data Hazards (RAW, WAR, WAW) using Data Forwarding

topics.name: COA: Control Hazards & Branch Penalties
learningObjectives: Analyze Control Hazards and Branch Penalties
Understand Branch Prediction and Delayed Branches

topics.name: COA: Memory Hierarchy & Locality of Reference
learningObjectives: Understand Memory Hierarchy & Access speeds
Master Temporal & Spatial Locality of Reference

topics.name: COA: Cache Memory Mapping (Direct Mapping)
learningObjectives: Master Direct cache mapping address division
Calculate Cache index, tag, and offsets

topics.name: COA: Cache Memory Mapping (Associative & Set-Associative)
learningObjectives: Understand Fully Associative mapping
Master Set-Associative mapping address division

topics.name: COA: Cache Policies & Virtual Memory
learningObjectives: Differentiate Write-Through vs Write-Back policies
Understand Virtual Memory, Paging, and Translation Lookaside Buffers (TLB)

topics.name: COA: Input-Output Organization (Programmed & Interrupt I/O)
learningObjectives: Analyze Programmed I/O vs Interrupt-Driven I/O modes
Master Daisy-Chaining priority systems

topics.name: COA: Direct Memory Access (DMA)
learningObjectives: Understand DMA controllers and register architectures
Differentiate DMA Burst transfer vs Cycle Stealing

topics.name: Process Management and Threading
learningObjectives: Understand Process Control Blocks and state transitions
Trace fork() execution trees and count child processes
Differentiate user-level vs kernel-level threads

topics.name: CPU Scheduling
learningObjectives: Calculate turnaround, waiting, and response times
Apply FCFS, SJF, SRTF, Round Robin, and Priority scheduling
Understand Gantt chart scheduling states

topics.name: Process Synchronization
learningObjectives: Resolve Mutual Exclusion, Progress, and Bounded Waiting
Implement solutions using Test-and-Set and Semaphores
Trace classic synchronization problems: Producer-Consumer, Dining Philosophers

topics.name: Deadlocks
learningObjectives: Learn deadlock conditions: mutual exclusion, hold-and-wait, no preemption, circular wait
Run Banker's Algorithm for resource allocation and safety checking
Understand deadlock detection and recovery strategies

topics.name: Memory Management
learningObjectives: Calculate page tables, frame sizes, and physical addresses
Master multi-level paging and translation lookaside buffers (TLB)
Understand segmentation memory structures

topics.name: Virtual Memory and Page Replacement
learningObjectives: Trace page replacements: FIFO, LRU, Optimal
Identify Belady's Anomaly behavior
Understand thrashing and working-set models

topics.name: File Systems and Disk Scheduling
learningObjectives: Understand file allocation tables, inodes, and directories
Calculate disk seek overheads
Apply FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN disk scheduling algorithms

topics.name: ER Model: Introduction to DBMS & Entities
learningObjectives: Introduction to Database Management Systems
Understand Entities, Attributes, and Entity Sets

topics.name: ER Model: Relationships & Mapping Cardinalities
learningObjectives: Master Relationship Sets & Mapping Cardinalities
Understand Total & Partial Participation Constraints

topics.name: ER Model: Weak Entity Sets & Table Conversion
learningObjectives: Design Weak Entity Sets & Identifying Relationships
Minimize ER Diagrams to Relational Tables

topics.name: Relational Model: Introduction & Relational Algebra Operators
learningObjectives: Learn Relational Model Concepts & Codd's Rules
Master Selection & Projection Operations

topics.name: Relational Model: Set Operations & Cartesian Product
learningObjectives: Master Union, Intersection, and Set Difference
Understand Rename & Cartesian Product

topics.name: Relational Model: Joins & Division Operators
learningObjectives: Master Inner, Outer, Theta, and Natural Joins
Understand Relational Algebra Division

topics.name: Relational Model: Tuple & Domain Calculus
learningObjectives: Understand Tuple Relational Calculus (TRC)
Understand Domain Relational Calculus (DRC)

topics.name: SQL: DDL Commands & Basic Queries
learningObjectives: Master SQL DDL Commands (Create, Drop, Alter, Truncate)
Write basic Select, Where, and logical operator queries

topics.name: SQL: Aggregations & Grouping Clauses
learningObjectives: Understand SQL Aggregate functions
Master Group By & Having clauses

topics.name: SQL: Joins, Nested Subqueries & Null Values
learningObjectives: Write SQL Joins & Nested/Correlated Subqueries
Understand SQL Null values & 3-valued logic

topics.name: Normalization: Integrity Constraints & FDs
learningObjectives: Master Primary Key, Foreign Key, and Referential Integrity
Calculate Functional Dependency closures

topics.name: Normalization: Equivalence, Canonical Covers & Keys
learningObjectives: Test FD Equivalence & calculate Canonical Cover
Calculate Super Keys & Candidate Keys

topics.name: Normalization: Normal Forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF, BCNN)
learningObjectives: Understand 1NF and 2NF criteria
Differentiate 3NF and BCNF properties

topics.name: Normalization: Lossless Join & Dependency Preservation
learningObjectives: Test for Lossless-Join Decomposition
Test for Dependency-Preserving Decomposition

topics.name: Transactions: ACID Properties & Schedules
learningObjectives: Learn Transaction states & ACID properties
Understand serial vs non-serial Schedules

topics.name: Transactions: Serializability (Conflict & View)
learningObjectives: Master Conflict Serializability & Precedence graphs
Understand View Serializability and testing

topics.name: Transactions: Recoverability & Concurrency Locking
learningObjectives: Differentiate Recoverable, Cascade-less, and Strict schedules
Master 2-Phase Locking (2PL), Strict 2PL, and Rigorous 2PL

topics.name: Transactions: Timestamping & Deadlocks
learningObjectives: Understand Timestamp Ordering & Thomas Write Rule
Manage Deadlocks (Prevention, Detection, Recovery)

topics.name: Indexing: File Structures & Single-Level Indexes
learningObjectives: Learn Heap, Sequential, and Hash File organization
Understand Dense, Sparse, Primary, and Secondary indexing

topics.name: Indexing: B-Trees and B+ Trees
learningObjectives: Master B-Tree insertion and deletion
Understand B+ Tree structures and average access times

topics.name: Data Link Layer Protocols
learningObjectives: Analyze sliding window protocols: Stop-and-Wait, Go-Back-N, Selective Repeat
Master framing, parity, CRC error detection, and Hamming codes
Calculate channel efficiency and transmission delays

topics.name: Medium Access Control
learningObjectives: Analyze ALOHA, Slotted ALOHA, CSMA/CD, and CSMA/CA protocols
Calculate backoff periods, minimum frame size, and collision zones
Understand Ethernet bridging, switches, and VLAN networks

topics.name: Routing Protocols
learningObjectives: Differentiate Distance Vector routing (RIP) and Link State routing (OSPF)
Solve Count-to-Infinity routing loop problems
Understand Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) routing

topics.name: IP Addressing and Subnetting
learningObjectives: Master Classful vs Classless IPv4 CIDR addressing
Calculate subnets, hosts, and range boundaries
Understand Network Address Translation (NAT) and IPv6 architectures

topics.name: Transport Layer TCP and UDP
learningObjectives: Understand TCP 3-way handshake and connection teardown
Master TCP Congestion Control: slow start, congestion avoidance, fast retransmit, fast recovery
Analyze TCP window sizes, flow control, and UDP sockets

topics.name: Application Layer Protocols
learningObjectives: Understand DNS query flows (iterative vs recursive)
Master SMTP, HTTP (persistent vs non-persistent), FTP, and DHCP
Analyze network port allocations

topics.name: Propositional and First-Order Logic
learningObjectives: Evaluate logical truth tables, tautologies, and contradictions
Master first-order logic quantifiers and translations
Apply rules of inference and logical deductions

topics.name: Sets, Relations, and Functions
learningObjectives: Analyze equivalence relations and partition mappings
Understand partial orders, Posets, Hasse diagrams, and Lattices
Solve functions: one-to-one, onto, composition, inverse counts

topics.name: Group Theory
learningObjectives: Identify Monoids, Semigroups, Groups, and Abelian groups
Calculate subgroup structures and Lagrange's theorem partitions
Determine cyclic groups and generators

topics.name: Combinatorics and Recurrence Relations
learningObjectives: Apply pigeonhole principle and inclusion-exclusion
Solve linear recurrence relations with boundary states
Generate sequences using Generating Functions

topics.name: Discrete Graph Theory
learningObjectives: Calculate graph degrees, handshaking lemma paths
Master planar graphs, Euler's formula, chromatic coloring counts
Understand Eulerian, Hamiltonian trails, matching, and vertex covers

topics.name: Linear Algebra
learningObjectives: Calculate determinants, matrix rank, and inverse systems
Solve systems of linear equations (consistent vs inconsistent)
Find eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and run LU decomposition

topics.name: Calculus
learningObjectives: Solve limits, continuity, and differentiability
Apply Mean Value Theorems: Rolle's, Lagrange's, Cauchy's
Evaluate maxima, minima, single, and double integrals

topics.name: Probability and Statistics
learningObjectives: Apply conditional probability and Bayes' Theorem
Master random variables, expectation, variance
Calculate probability distributions: Normal, Exponential, Binomial, Poisson

topics.name: Quantitative Aptitude
learningObjectives: Calculate time and work, time-speed-distance
Solve percentages, ratios, averages, profit & loss
Evaluate permutations, combinations, and basic geometry in aptitude

topics.name: Verbal Aptitude
learningObjectives: Master English grammar, vocabulary, synonyms, antonyms
Understand sentence completion and logical relationships
Solve reading comprehension and word usage

topics.name: Logical and Spatial Aptitude
learningObjectives: Master pattern recognition, series, calendars, clocks
Solve Venn Diagrams and syllogisms
Understand spatial folding, rotating, and scaling shapes
